cases.question:
Context:
Earlier in this conversation you created a reply draft:
$ spark draft --reply-to 5678 --body 'Thanks for the update - Friday works.'
Output:
ID: 9012
Link: https://sparkmailapp.com/dpl/bl?token=EXAMPLE9012

User request: Actually, make it shorter and mention I'll bring the metrics doc.
expected_behavior: Agent updates the existing draft with `spark draft --edit 9012`
Agent does not create a new draft with --reply-to or --to

cases.question: User request: Draft an email to alice@example.com with subject 'Q3 kickoff agenda' covering three agenda points: budget review, hiring plan, and the migration timeline. Link to the planning doc at https://example.com/plan.
expected_behavior: Agent composes the body as markdown: a list for the three agenda points and a markdown link to the planning doc
Agent uses $'...' shell quoting so newlines are real
Agent addresses alice@example.com with the requested subject

cases.question:
Context:
$ spark thread 7712
Thread: 'Partnership proposal' (3 messages)
--
ID: 7712 | From: alice@example.com | 2026-07-14 | 'Here is the proposal...'
ID: 7745 | From: me@example.com | 2026-07-15 | 'Looking it over now.'
ID: 7788 | From: alice@example.com | 2026-07-18 | 'Any decision on the proposal?'

User request: Reply to Alice that the proposal looks good and we're in.
expected_behavior: Agent replies with `spark draft --reply-to 7788`, the last message in the thread
Agent does not start a new thread

cases.question:
Context:
$ spark search --filter "to:bob@example.com subject:contract"
ID: 6601 | From: me@example.com | To: bob@example.com | 2026-07-15 | 'Contract redlines attached - can you confirm by Friday?'
(no reply since; today is 2026-07-20)

User request: Bump this - Bob never responded.
expected_behavior: Agent bumps by replying to message 6601, the user's own last outgoing message, via --reply-to
Agent does not compose a new cold email with --to

cases.question:
Context:
$ spark emails --filter "from:carol@example.com is:unread"
ID: 3321 | From: carol@example.com | 2026-07-20 | 'Status on the contract?'

User request: Draft a reply saying legal is reviewing and we'll have it Thursday. I want to review before anything goes out.
expected_behavior: Agent creates a reply draft to message 3321
Agent does not run `spark action send` - the user asked to review first

cases.question:
Context:
You just ran:
$ spark draft --reply-to 3321 --body 'Legal is reviewing...'
Output:
ID: 4470
Link: https://sparkmailapp.com/dpl/bl?token=EXAMPLE4470

User request: (Report back to the user that the draft is ready.)
expected_behavior: Agent's report includes the Spark deep link as a clickable markdown link

cases.question:
Context:
Today is Monday 2026-07-20. Draft ID 4411 is ready and the user has already approved its content.

User request: Great - send it tomorrow at 9am.
expected_behavior: Agent schedules with `spark action send 4411 --date 2026-07-21T09:00` using the CLI's T-separated date format

cases.question:
Context:
This is a NEW session; nothing has been run yet. Yesterday, in a previous session, a draft to alice@example.com about pricing was created, but its ID is not available in this conversation. The user's account is me@example.com.

User request: Update that pricing draft to add a section on volume discounts.
expected_behavior: Agent lists the Drafts folder to recover the draft ID before editing
Agent does not compose a new email with `draft --to`

cases.question: User request: Draft an email to matt@example.com, subject 'Setup notes', laying out three numbered sections. Section 1 'Foundation' should have a sentence of intro plus two sub-bullets under it, section 2 'While coding' one sentence, section 3 'Before merging' one sentence.
expected_behavior: Agent writes each section as `1. **Foundation**` - list marker first, emphasis inside the item
Agent does NOT write `**1. Foundation**`, which renders as a literal typed number with no list
Content nested under a numbered item is indented three spaces so it lands inside the <li>

cases.question:
Context:
$ spark accounts
Email Account: me@gmail.com (Access: triage)

User request: Put a draft in my gmail to matt@example.com about the Q3 timeline. No need to send it.
expected_behavior: Agent drafts through `spark draft` - the account is Gmail but the client is Spark
Agent does not use gog/gog-safe or the Gmail API to create the draft
Agent does not mix clients on the same message

cases.question:
Context:
You drafted an email whose body used markdown links, then read it back:
$ spark thread 4470
  Details are in the [deploy runbook](https://example.com/runbook).
  Ping me if Friday is a problem.

User request: Did my links actually convert to real hyperlinks, or is the markdown broken?
expected_behavior: Agent explains the links DID convert - `spark thread` prints flattened plain text and re-serializes anchors as [text](url)
Agent does not report that the markdown failed to convert
Agent notes that reading the stored HTML through the provider is the only way to see real structure

cases.question: User request: Draft a note to alice@example.com pointing her at the status page, https://example.com/status, and telling her it updates every 5 minutes.
expected_behavior: Agent wraps the URL in a markdown link with named text, e.g. [status page](https://example.com/status)
Agent does not paste the bare URL, which the renderer leaves as unlinked plain text

cases.question: User request: Draft an email to matt@example.com walking through our four-stage review process, one short paragraph per stage.
expected_behavior: Agent uses bold lines or a numbered list for the stage headers
Agent does NOT use markdown headings (# / ##), tables, or --- rules - the default house style is email that reads hand-typed
Agent still formats the body rather than sending a plain wall of text

cases.question:
Context:
$ spark accounts
Email Account: me@gmail.com (Access: triage)

notes@mycompany.org is a send-as alias on that Gmail account.

User request: Draft a note to client@example.com from my notes@mycompany.org address about the invoice.
expected_behavior: Agent passes --account notes@mycompany.org on the draft creation command
Agent does not try to set the sender via --edit afterwards, which is silently ignored
Agent does not reach for a Gmail CLI to get the alias
